perturbation.original_text: E. You pay us a nonrefundable Initial Franchise Fee of ________________ _______________________ Dollars ($________) which is payable in full on the date of this Agreement. The Initial Franchise Fee has been fully earned upon receipt and is nonrefundable in consideration of the expenses incurred by us in granting this franchise and for the lost or deferred opportunity to franchise others.
perturbation.changed_text: E. You pay us a nonrefundable Initial Franchise Fee of ________________ _______________________ Dollars ($________) which is payable in full on the date of this Agreement. The Initial Franchise Fee will be refundable within the first 30 days of signing, if the franchisee decides not to proceed, but is otherwise nonrefundable in consideration of the expenses incurred by us in granting this franchise and for the lost or deferred opportunity to franchise others.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating the Initial Franchise Fee is refundable within the first 30 days in Paragraph I.E, but paragraph I.A states that location will be designated and approved. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the refundability of the franchise fee if the location isn't approved within the first 30 days of signing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the provisions stated below, we hereby grant you a license to use the "Buffalo Wild Wings" Marks and System, and you undertake the obligation to operate a Buffalo Wild Wings restaurant facility featuring the Menu Items and providing sit-down, carry-out and other restaurant services ("Franchised 1 Restaurant"), and to use the Marks solely in connection with the System, as it is currently established, and as it may be changed, improved and further developed from time to time, at one (1) location only, such location to be: 1) ____________________________________________________________________________ ___________________________________________________________________________, or 2) at a location to be designated, as provided in Paragraph III within the area described on EXHIBIT B. When a location has been designated and approved by you and us, it will become part of Paragraph I.A.1., as if originally incorporated in that Paragraph. You shall not relocate your Franchised Restaurant without our prior written approval.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction. The original text states the Initial Franchise Fee is nonrefundable, but the changed text allows for a refund within 30 days. The contradicted text Paragraph I.A states that location will be designated and approved. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the refundability of the franchise fee if the location isn't approved within the first 30 days of signing. This is a clear inconsistency as per definition 5 in the Perturbation Definitions, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: D. We shall give you written notice of our election not to renew the franchise at least three (3) months prior to the expiration of the initial or first renewal term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: D. We shall give you written notice of our election not to renew the franchise at least one (1) month prior to the expiration of the initial or first renewal term of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the notification period for non-renewal from three months to one month in Section II.D, this introduces an in-text contradiction with Section II.B.3 which requires franchisees to provide written notice of their desire to renew at least six months prior to the end of the term. This contradiction creates confusion about the actual timeframe for renewal-related communications, potentially leading to disputes and enforcement uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You have given us written notice of your desire to renew at least six (6) months but not more than twelve (12) months prior to the end of the term;
perturbation.justification: The change from a three-month to a one-month notice period for non-renewal directly contradicts the six-month notice period required for franchisees to express their desire to renew. This creates a clear conflict regarding the timing of renewal-related communications, as outlined in Section II.D (changed from 'three (3) months' to 'one (1) month') and the contradicted text ('You have given us written notice of your desire to renew at least six (6) months'). This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: A. You will pay us without offset, credit or deduction of any nature, so long as this Agreement is in effect, a monthly Continuing Fee equal to five percent (5%) of the Gross Sales derived from the Franchised Restaurant.
perturbation.changed_text: A. You will pay us without offset, credit or deduction of any nature, so long as this Agreement is in effect, a monthly Continuing Fee equal to seven percent (7%) of the Gross Sales derived from the Franchised Restaurant.
perturbation.explanation: By increasing the Continuing Fee from 5% to 7% in Section X.A, this creates an in-text contradiction with Section II.B.5, which states that the percentage Continuing Fee shall not exceed seven percent (7%) during any renewal period. This contradiction introduces uncertainty and legal ambiguity as to the applicable Continuing Fee amount during renewal periods versus the initial term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You have executed for the renewal term our then-current form of Franchise Agreement (with appropriate modifications to reflect the fact that the agreement relates to the grant of a renewal franchise), which shall supersede in all respects this Agreement, and the terms of which may differ from the terms of this Agreement, including, without limitation, a different percentage Continuing Fee and advertising contribution; provided, however, that the percentage Continuing Fee shall not exceed seven percent (7%) during any renewal period. You will not be required to pay the then-current initial franchise fee or its equivalent;
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 5% Continuing Fee. The changed text increases this to 7%. The contradicted text states that the Continuing Fee shall not exceed 7% during any renewal period. Although the new fee is within the limit stated in the contradicted text, it creates a contradiction. Since the initial agreement now specifies a 7% fee, and the renewal period is capped at 7%, there is no room for negotiation of any increased fee during renewal. Thus, the change introduces an inconsistency, and makes a direct contradiction between the initial fee and the allowance of the fee not exceeding 7% during the renewal period, according to the Perturbation Explanation and the definitions above.
